title: CatBoost Native Categorical Ensemble
summary: The best solutions rely on AutoGluon GBM/XGB.
rationale: The best solutions rely on AutoGluon GBM/XGB. CatBoost’s ordered target statistics are specifically designed for high-cardinality categorical features without leakage, which is relevant for Driver, Race, and crossed categorical pit-window features. Public tabular guidance also finds GBM/XGB/CatBoost ensembles often have complementary errors.
implementation_hint: Train a standalone CatBoostClassifier with raw categorical columns plus engineered numeric features, using race-aware validation if feasible. Blend or rank-average CatBoost probabilities with the existing AutoGluon GBM/XGB predictions, tuning only a few weights on OOF predictions.
expected_effect: Likely modest AUC gain from different categorical treatment and model diversity.
risk: CatBoost can overfit if random holdout shares the same races; use ordered boosting defaults and validate by Race/Year where possible.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
